src: οὐ γὰρ ἐφ᾽ ἑνὶ τόπῳ οὐδὲ ἐν βραχεῖ σχοινίσματι
lit: For not on one place nor in a small tract
sm: For not in one place, nor in a small territory,

syn.mc: Initial negated prepositional phrase of a main assertion completed in line 312.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: No finite verb in this line; the negated locative phrases prepare for καθίδρυται in line 312.
syn.rhet: Negated parallelism opens a contrast with the universal diffusion in lines 313-314.

src: ἡ τοῦ θεοῦ δόξα καθίδρυται,
lit: The glory of God has been established,
sm: the glory of God has been established,

syn.mc: Main clause completing the negated locative contrast of line 311.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ τοῦ θεοῦ δόξα. Verb: καθίδρυται.
syn.rhet: The clause denies local confinement of divine glory.

src: ἀλλ᾽ ἐπὶ πάντα τὰ πέρατα τῆς οἰκουμένης
lit: But upon all the ends of the inhabited world
sm: but over all the ends of the inhabited world

syn.mc: Adversative prepositional phrase completed by the verb in line 314.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: No finite verb in this line; it prepares the positive counterpart to line 311.
syn.rhet: Universalizing spatial contrast answers the earlier negations.

src: ἐκκέχυται ἡ χάρις αὐτοῦ,
lit: His grace has been poured out,
sm: his grace has been poured out,

syn.mc: Main clause completing the adversative spatial contrast of line 313.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ χάρις αὐτοῦ. Verb: ἐκκέχυται.
syn.rhet: The imagery shifts from fixed establishment to outpoured diffusion.

src: καὶ ἐνταῦθα κατεσκήνωκεν ὁ παντοκράτωρ θεὸς
lit: And here the almighty God has taken up dwelling
sm: and here the almighty God has taken up his dwelling

syn.mc: Coordinate main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ παντοκράτωρ θεός. Verb: κατεσκήνωκεν. Adverb: ἐνταῦθα.
syn.rhet: Universal diffusion is paradoxically paired with present local indwelling.

src: διὰ Χριστοῦ ᾿Ϊησοῦ"
lit: Through Christ Jesus.
sm: through Christ Jesus.
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ᾿Ϊησοῦ -> Ἰησοῦ. Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Prepositional phrase dependent on κατεσκήνωκεν.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Instrumental/mediating phrase: διὰ Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ modifies the indwelling of God.
syn.rhet: Christological mediation seals the universal-local argument.

src: ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας. ἀμήν.
lit: To whom [be] the glory unto the ages. Amen.
sm: To him be glory unto the ages. Amen.

syn.mc: Elliptical doxological clause.
syn.sub: The relative pronoun links back to Christ Jesus.
syn.svo: Dative referent: ᾧ. Subject: ἡ δόξα. Implied predicate such as ἔστω or ἐστίν. Prepositional phrase: εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας.
syn.rhet: A brief doxology closes the paragraph before a new exposition begins.

src: Τὸ μὲν οὖν διήγημα τοῦ τύπου καὶ τῆς ἀνταποδόσεως ἀκηκόατε"
ana: Τὸ μὲν οὖν διήγημα τοῦ τύπου καὶ τῆς ἀνταποδόσεως ἀκηκόατε·
lit: The narrative of the type and of the correspondence, therefore, you have heard;
sm: So then, you have heard the narrative of the type and its correspondence;
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Main clause addressing the audience.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; μὲν anticipates the contrasting imperative of line 320.
syn.svo: Object: Τὸ ... διήγημα. Verb: ἀκηκόατε. Subject: implied ὑμεῖς.
syn.rhet: A resumptive audience address marks the shift from prior exposition to fresh teaching.

src: ἀκούσατε καὶ τὴν κατασκευὴν τοῦ μυστηρίου"
ana: ἀκούσατε καὶ τὴν κατασκευὴν τοῦ μυστηρίου·
lit: Hear also the construction of the mystery;
sm: hear also the structure of the mystery;
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Imperative main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 321 gives the question this imperative answers.
syn.svo: Verb: ἀκούσατε. Object: τὴν κατασκευὴν τοῦ μυστηρίου. Subject: implied ὑμεῖς.
syn.rhet: The hearers are led from narrative to inner structure.

src: τί ἐστιν τὸ πάσχα;
lit: What is the Passover?
sm: What is the Passover?

syn.mc: Direct interrogative clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Predicate-question word: τί. Verb: ἐστιν. Subject: τὸ πάσχα.
syn.rhet: A catechetical question opens the etymological explanation.

src: ἀπὸ γὰρ τοῦ συμβεβηκότος τὸ ὄνομα κέκληται"
ana: ἀπὸ γὰρ τοῦ συμβεβηκότος τὸ ὄνομα κέκληται·
tok: pf act ptcp gen sg n
lit: For the name has been called from what has happened;
sm: For the name has been derived from what happened;
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Main explanatory clause answering line 321.
syn.sub: The articular participle τοῦ συμβεβηκότος functions as a substantive genitive after ἀπό.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ ὄνομα. Verb: κέκληται. Prepositional phrase: ἀπὸ τοῦ συμβεβηκότος.
syn.ptc: συμβεβηκότος is substantival in the genitive, meaning 'the event that happened'.
syn.rhet: The answer proceeds by etymology through event-name correspondence.

src: ἀπὸ τοῦ παθεῖν τὸ πάσχειν.
lit: From the suffering comes the 'to suffer'.
sm: The word 'passion' comes from 'to suffer'.

syn.mc: Elliptical explanatory phrase completing line 322.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the articular infinitive τοῦ παθεῖν depends on ἀπό.
syn.svo: Prepositional etymological phrase: ἀπὸ τοῦ παθεῖν. Object-like infinitive phrase: τὸ πάσχειν.
syn.rhet: A compact paronomasia explains πάσχα/πάσχειν through cognate sound and sense.

src: μάθετε οὖν τίς ὁ πάσχων,
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: Learn therefore who the suffering one is,
sm: Learn, then, who the sufferer is,

syn.mc: Imperative main clause with indirect question.
syn.sub: τίς ὁ πάσχων forms an indirect interrogative object of μάθετε.
syn.svo: Verb: μάθετε. Subject: implied ὑμεῖς. Indirect-question content: τίς ὁ πάσχων.
syn.ptc: πάσχων is substantival with the article: 'the sufferer'.
syn.rhet: A catechetical imperative turns from etymology to identification.

src: καὶ τίς ὃ τῷ πάσχοντι συμπαθῶν,
ana: καὶ τίς ὁ τῷ πάσχοντι συμπαθῶν,
tok: pres act ptcp dat sg m
pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And who is the one sympathizing with the sufferer,
sm: and who is the one who shares the sufferer's suffering,
emd: The raw ὃ is analyzed as ὁ, since the article is required before the substantival participle.

syn.mc: Coordinate indirect question dependent on μάθετε.
syn.sub: No new finite clause; τίς ὁ ... συμπαθῶν is the second object-content of μάθετε.
syn.svo: Interrogative subject phrase: τίς ὁ ... συμπαθῶν. Dative complement: τῷ πάσχοντι.
syn.ptc: πάσχοντι and συμπαθῶν are both substantival participles, the latter governing the dative.
syn.rhet: The paired questions distinguish the sufferer from the one in solidarity with him.

src: καὶ διὰ τί πάρεστιν ὁ κύριος ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς
lit: And because of what is the Lord present upon the earth
sm: And why is the Lord present on the earth

syn.mc: Third interrogative clause dependent on μάθετε.
syn.sub: The purpose clause follows in lines 329-330.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ κύριος. Verb: πάρεστιν. Interrogative phrase: διὰ τί. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς.
syn.rhet: The catechetical sequence shifts from identity to divine purpose.

src: ἵνα τὸν πάσχοντα ἀμφιασάμενος
tok: pres act ptcp acc sg m
aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: In order that, having clothed himself with the sufferer,
sm: in order that, after clothing himself with the sufferer,

syn.mc: Purpose clause introduced by ἵνα and completed in line 330.
syn.sub: ἵνα introduces the telic clause. The participle ἀμφιασάμενος is subordinate within it.
syn.svo: Implied subject: ὁ κύριος. Participle: ἀμφιασάμενος. Object: τὸν πάσχοντα.
syn.ptc: ἀμφιασάμενος is circumstantial within the purpose clause, expressing the mode of redemptive action.
syn.rhet: The Lord's solidarity is cast as vesting himself in the sufferer.

src: ἁρπάσῃ εἰς τὰ ὕψηλα τῶν οὐρανῶν.
lit: He might snatch [him] into the heights of the heavens.
sm: he might snatch him up into the heights of the heavens.

syn.mc: Verb of the purpose clause begun in line 329.
syn.sub: Still under ἵνα.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ κύριος, implied. Verb: ἁρπάσῃ. Object: implied τὸν πάσχοντα. Prepositional phrase: εἰς τὰ ὕψηλα τῶν οὐρανῶν.
syn.rhet: The telos of incarnation and solidarity is upward rescue.

src: Ο θεὸς ἐν ἀρχῇ ποιήσας τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ τὴν γῆν
ana: Ὁ θεὸς ἐν ἀρχῇ ποιήσας τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ τὴν γῆν
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: God, in the beginning having made the heaven and the earth,
sm: God, having made heaven and earth in the beginning,

syn.mc: Subject phrase with participial lead-in for the creation narrative.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the participle prepares the main verbs in lines 333-335.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ θεός. Participle: ποιήσας. Object: τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ τὴν γῆν. Prepositional phrase: ἐν ἀρχῇ.
syn.ptc: ποιήσας is antecedent action introducing God's creative acts.
syn.rhet: A new scriptural retelling begins with compressed Genesis diction.

src: καὶ πάντα τὰ ἐν αὐτοῖς διὰ τοῦ λόγου,
lit: And all the things in them through the Word,
sm: and everything in them through the Word,

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the participial object of line 331.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Additional object under ποιήσας is understood: πάντα τὰ ἐν αὐτοῖς. Instrumental phrase: διὰ τοῦ λόγου.
syn.rhet: The creation statement is expanded christologically through διὰ τοῦ λόγου.

src: ἀνεπλάσατο ἀπὸ τῆς γῆς τὸν ἄνθρωπον
lit: He fashioned man from the earth
sm: he fashioned man from the earth

syn.mc: Main clause of the creation narrative.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ θεός, understood from line 331. Verb: ἀνεπλάσατο. Object: τὸν ἄνθρωπον. Prepositional phrase: ἀπὸ τῆς γῆς.
syn.rhet: The narrative narrows from cosmic creation to human formation.

src: καὶ ἰδίαν πνοὴν μετέδωκεν
lit: And he imparted his own breath
sm: and he imparted his own breath

syn.mc: Coordinate main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: God, understood. Verb: μετέδωκεν. Object: ἰδίαν πνοήν.
syn.rhet: The wording emphasizes intimacy between creator and creature.

src: τοῦτον δὲ ἔθετο εἰς τὸν παράδεισον κατὰ ἀνατολὰς
lit: And he placed him into paradise toward the east
sm: and he placed him in paradise toward the east

syn.mc: Coordinate main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: God, understood. Verb: ἔθετο. Object: τοῦτον. Prepositional phrases: εἰς τὸν παράδεισον, κατὰ ἀνατολάς.
syn.rhet: The human story is relocated from creation to ordered dwelling.

src: ἐν "Ἔδεμ, ἐκεῖ τρυφᾶν,

sm: in Eden, there to delight,

syn.svo: Prepositional phrase: ἐν Ἔδεμ. Purpose infinitive: τρυφᾶν. Adverb: ἐκεῖ.
syn.rhet: The phrase telescopes place and purpose into a compact paradise formula.

src: τάδε αὐτῷ νομοθετήσας διὰ τῆς ἐντολῆς"

syn.svo: Subject: God, understood. Participle: νομοθετήσας. Object: τάδε. Indirect object: αὐτῷ. Prepositional phrase: διὰ τῆς ἐντολῆς.

src: Ἀπὸ παντὸς ξύλου βρώσει φάγετε,
lit: From every tree, with eating, eat,
sm: From every tree you may freely eat,

syn.mc: Quoted imperative clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Verb: φάγετε. Prepositional phrase: Ἀπὸ παντὸς ξύλου. Dative βρώσει intensifies or qualifies the command idiomatically.
syn.rhet: Biblical pleonasm/idiom gives solemn force to the permission.

src: ἀπὸ δὲ τοῦ ξύλου τοῦ γινώσκειν ἀγαθὸν καὶ πονηρὸν

sm: But from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil

syn.svo: No finite verb in this line; the prepositional phrase prepares οὐ φάγεσθε in line 341.
